extra_model_fields.notes_for_reviewer_on_resubmission: The revised submission will not invent missing numeric inputs. If the planned-withdrawal vector remains unavailable, the admin-limit check will be shown as 'cannot verify' and accompanied by parameterized/hypothetical examples.
validation_steps_before_resubmission: Ensure memo explicitly lists missing packet fields and does not state 'no missing fields synthesized'.
Verify spreadsheet has an empty/planned-withdrawal input table and admin-check cells driven only from that table.
Confirm hypothetical examples are located on a separate sheet and are labelled 'HYPOTHETICAL'.
Run a quick smoke-open test to ensure formulas reference the input range and produce pass/fail outputs without any hard-coded assumed values.

planned_changes: Edit startup_authorization_memo.md: remove the asserted assumption that planned-withdrawal vector was provided; add explicit statement that the packet did not include a planned-withdrawal schedule and therefore the single-bank admin-limit check cannot be verified without that input.

In startup_authorization_memo.md, add a short 'How to verify admin-limit' section that shows the admin-check as a conditional expression and documents the spreadsheet input cells to populate for verification.

Modify rod_worth_review.xlsx: add a dedicated input range/sheet named '04_Planned_Withdrawals' with empty/default input cells for each bank's planned withdrawal. Implement admin-limit pass/fail cells that compute per-bank comparisons against admin_limit_single_bank_withdrawal_pcm using only those input cells.

Move the prior assumed admin-check calculations (where planned_withdrawal = conservative_bank_worth) into a new sheet 'Hypothetical_Scenarios' and label them clearly as examples only; do not mix them with verified input/results.

Update the spreadsheet and memo cross-references so the memo points to the exact cell ranges for the planned-withdrawal inputs and the admin-check result cells, enabling deterministic verification by graders.

Add a one-line run-note in the memo stating the spreadsheet was generated via the OOXML writer due to environment library constraints (openpyxl unavailable) so verifiers are aware of the generation method.
Re-run any sensitivity checks only using either verified inputs or clearly labeled hypothetical inputs; ensure all hypothetical scenarios are isolated and annotated.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Remove disallowed invention of missing numeric input (planned-withdrawal) and make admin-limit checks verifiable or clearly hypothetical to comply with rubric.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The memo and workbook contain correct formulas and traceable calculations for conservative bank worth and shutdown margins, but the memo synthesizes a missing numeric input (planned-withdrawal vector) by assuming planned_withdrawal = conservative_bank_worth. Per rubric this is a disqualifying error: do not invent missing numeric inputs. Instead explicitly mark the admin single-bank withdrawal check as unverifiable or present conditional/hypothetical checks clearly labeled as such.
